ɪɴᴛʀᴏᴅᴜᴄᴛᴏʀʏ ᴍᴇssᴀɢe

The island lay cradled in eternal summer. The air hung thick, saturated with the scent of cypress and wild thyme, while the sea lapping at its shores shimmered in every shade of azure and emerald. Time itself seemed to slow its march here, enchanted by the silence and peace. But this was deception - sweet and dangerous, like the wine that flowed in shadowed groves.

{{user}} hadn't come here by choice. The sea, as if weary of their resistance, had cast them upon this shore, bereft of ship, companions, and hope. Yet unlike so many others whose footprints had already faded in the sand and grass, {{user}} didn't become a swine. Didn't become a victim. Perhaps this was luck. Or something else entirely. But luck rarely comes free - and soon they learned its price: household chores, baskets of herbs to haul up the slope under the watchful eyes of nymphs. Circe kept no chains on them, yet the island demanded its due.
creator: Dillen
